title: API, ReadOnly does not work
body:
### Issue Description

When using the REST API, ReadOnly for mounts does not seem to work. But the "compat" version _does_ work.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue

POST the following to "/v3.0.0/libpod/containers/create" or "/v4.0.0/libpod/containers/create"

{
  "command": [
    "/bin/bash"
  ],
  "image": "ubuntu:22.04",
  "mounts": [
    {
      "Destination": "/test/",
      "ReadOnly": true,
      "Source": "/test/",
      "Type": "bind"
    } 
  ],
  "name": "container0",
  "terminal": true
}

### Describe the results you received

/test is not read-only

### Describe the results you expected

A read-only folder /test

### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

Appart from
"ReadOnly": true

I tried, without success, other variants like 
"ReadOnly": "true"
"ReadOnly": "yes"

Strangely there are no apparent error messages when I write different variants of the command. I get error messages if I mistreat other parts of the API.

**NOTE: The "compat" version of the command works as expected.** 
Apart from the "ReadOnly" flag I have successfully used many different kinds of API calls.

comments.body:
The mount type that we use in our specgen format is: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/8b2adb836fd8657b3d8cf2685887e2bc080b79ae/vendor/github.com/opencontainers/runtime-spec/specs-go/config.go#L113-L127

So you need to parse the read only option in the options array
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I think this was solved by @Luap99 response. reopen if I am mistaken.
